name: ISLE
summary: Cranelift's typed term-rewrite DSL for instruction selection. Each rule is a term-rewrite from CLIF input patterns to machine-instruction outputs. The ISLE compiler merges all rules into a decision tree, sharing matching work across rules.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: ISLE (Instruction Selection / Lowering Expressions) is Cranelift's DSL for writing instruction-selection rules. Each rule is a typed term-rewrite from CLIF input patterns to machine-instruction outputs. The ISLE compiler merges all rules into a decision tree, sharing matching work across rules.

sections.title: Rules as a representation in their own right
sections.content:
The point: instruction selection rules are themselves a representation, and they should be readable, type-checked, and verifiable. Hand-written instruction selectors (LLVM SelectionDAG, GCC machine descriptions) are notoriously hard to maintain; ISLE makes them declarative.

The declarative rule shape opens the door to formal-verification work over instruction selection: rules can be model-checked, fuzzed against the underlying ISA semantics, and reasoned about as a term-rewrite system rather than a tangle of hand-written matchers.
